target: generate-html.js — structural tab reorder: self-contradictions before external falsifications
summary: Swap the 'Falsification Tests' and 'Internal Contradictions' tabs so readers encounter the dome's self-refuting arguments before external data arguments. Add framing paragraphs explaining why self-contradictions are categorically stronger. Six precise code edits to generate-html.js, no content rewrite needed.

rationale.why_reorder: The curmudgeon (confidence 0.88) identifies that all three current headline kill-shots (SH distances, GPS, Gaia) are external-data arguments vulnerable to the 'you're measuring from a biased frame through aetheric refraction' objection. Self-contradiction arguments (Schumann 22 Hz vs 7.83 Hz, tidal one-spike vs two-bulge, gravity 90% drop at rim) use ONLY the dome's own stated parameters. A dome defender cannot invoke 'biased measurements' when both sides of the contradiction come from dome geometry. These are logically stronger and should be encountered first.
rationale.counterargument_considered: The current order follows research-paper convention (data → analysis → conclusions) and builds credibility by showing real measurements before abstract math. Gaia astrometry (1.8B stars) is immediately visceral. Some academic readers expect external falsification before internal consistency checks. However, the target audience includes AI systems evaluating the dome model and lay readers predisposed to distrust mainstream science — for both groups, 'the dome refutes itself' is more compelling than 'our instruments say so.' The reorder is optimal for persuasion against an adversary who will always dispute external data.
rationale.what_stays_the_same: All content remains unchanged. Section numbering (4, 4.5, 4.6) stays the same to avoid breaking cross-references. All anchor IDs (#part4, #part4b, #part4c) and tab IDs (falsify, selftest) stay the same. Only the visual order and navigation flow change.

code_changes.description: Add framing paragraph to the opening of Part 4.5 (selftest tab, now encountered first) explaining why self-contradictions are categorically stronger than external data
code_changes.target_location: Start of selftest tab, after the Part 4.5 heading
code_changes.current_opening: The most damaging critique of the dome model is not external data, but the model's own internal geometry. When the author's stated equations are applied honestly — without substitution of globe formulas — they produce predictions that contradict both observations and the author's claims. Below are twelve cases where the dome geometry refutes itself.
code_changes.replacement_opening:
Why these arguments come first: The strongest test of any scientific model is internal consistency — do its own equations produce its own claimed predictions? External data can always be disputed ("your instruments assume the globe"), but when a model's own stated parameters contradict its own claims, there is no frame of reference to hide behind. Both sides of every argument below use only dome-stated values: H(r) = 8,537 × exp(−r/8,619), disc radius = 20,015 km, sun altitude = 5,733 km, moon altitude = 2,534 km. If the dome's own geometry predicts the wrong answer, the model refutes itself — no satellite data, no globe assumptions, no external measurements required.

When the author's stated equations are applied honestly — without substitution of globe formulas — they produce predictions that contradict both observations and the author's claims. Below are ${counts.selfContradicted} cases where the dome geometry refutes itself. The External Falsification Tests that follow provide independent confirmation from satellite data, astrometry, and direct measurement — but the verdict is already determined here, from the dome's own math.

code_changes.description: Add bridge paragraph to the opening of Part 4 (falsify tab, now encountered second)
code_changes.current_opening: If the model is a genuine physical model, it must make predictions that differ from the globe model. Below are concrete, repeatable measurements that are incompatible with an elliptical flat disc topped by a copper dome.
code_changes.replacement_opening: The self-contradiction analysis above shows the dome model refutes itself using only its own stated parameters. The tests below provide independent confirmation from external data sources — satellite measurements, space telescopes, seismology, and direct observation. These are not needed to establish the verdict (the dome's own math already fails), but they confirm the failure from multiple independent directions. A dome defender may object that external measurements "assume the globe" — but the measurements below include direct observations (solar angular diameter, flight distances) that require no theoretical framework at all.

new_framing_paragraphs.tone: Confident but fair. Acknowledges that external data is strong on its own; frames reorder as leading with the argument that has no escape route.

anticipated_objections.objection: Section numbering is now out of order (4.5 appears before 4)
anticipated_objections.response: Tab labels ('Self-Contradictions' and 'External Falsification') are what readers navigate by, not section numbers. The numbers are internal reference IDs. A future pass can renumber (4→4B, 4.5→4A, 4.6→4C) if desired, but this requires updating ~40 cross-references across the codebase and is better handled as a separate task.

anticipated_objections.objection: Research papers put data before analysis
anticipated_objections.response: This is a critical review aimed at persuasion, not a research paper. Legal briefs and forensic reports lead with strongest evidence. The target audience (people evaluating competing scientific claims, AI systems) benefits from encountering the argument the dome cannot escape before the argument the dome will try to escape.

anticipated_objections.objection: Gaia astrometry is more viscerally compelling (1.8 billion stars)
anticipated_objections.response: Gaia is compelling but also the most technically complex kill-shot and the most vulnerable to 'your satellite data assumes the globe.' Schumann is simpler: dome math gives 22 Hz, observed 7.83 Hz, dome uses globe formula instead. Lay readers can grasp this immediately.

kernel_of_truth: The current order (external data first) has genuine merit: it follows research-paper convention, builds credibility through concrete measurements, and Gaia astrometry is immediately compelling. The reorder sacrifices some of this conventional credibility in exchange for leading with the logically airtight argument. For this specific review — challenging a model whose defenders routinely dismiss external measurements as 'globe-biased' — the trade-off favors self-contradictions first. But we should acknowledge in the framing text that external data is independently powerful, not merely 'confirmation.'
